message:
test(frankenpandas): extend prelude compile guard for fd90.182-203 (fd90.204 / br-cj8ys)

The prelude_completeness_compile_guard test was last updated for
fd90.155 and didn't reference items added since then. Its docstring
claims "If anyone removes one of these from the prelude, this test
refuses to compile" — but the test was silent on regressions of the
5 prelude additions made between fd90.182 and fd90.203.

Extended to cover:
- fd90.182: From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> for Scalar (.into() ergonomics)
- fd90.192: DataFrameColumnInput
- fd90.201: MultiIndexOrIndex
- fd90.202: ColumnError, ExprError, FrameError, IndexError, IoError
  (5 of the 7 error types added — TypeError + GroupByError were
  already exercised earlier in the test)
- fd90.203: ValidityMask

Also removed the stale `use crate::{GroupByError, TypeError};` import
and updated the docstring (those types are now in the prelude).

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -321,6 +321,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-cfld","title":"fd90.35: SqlColumnSchema::comment field for column-level comments","description":"Add SqlColumnSchema::comment: Option<String> field for full SQLAlchemy.Inspector.get_columns() parity (the dict return shape includes 'comment'). SQLite has no column-comment storage so the rusqlite table_schema impl always emits comment=None. PG/MySQL/MSSQL backends override their table_schema to populate it from pg_description / information_schema.columns / sys.extended_properties. 7 existing struct-literal sites bulk-updated with comment: None. Tests: SQLite returns None, PG-like backend stub returns Some. Stays a small surface change to SqlColumnSchema only — table-level comment already covered by fd90.32's table_comment trait method.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:43:14.720444782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:46:47.196344961Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:46:47.193815352Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ch4o","title":"[HIGH] fp-frame grouped-window flat-string label is not real MultiIndex (r4au)","description":"Reviewing 2f80719 (br-frankenpandas-r4au, cod): GroupByRolling::apply_grouped_rolling and GroupByResample paths now emit a flattened string label like 'IndexLabel::Utf8("(a, 2024-01)")' as a workaround for missing row MultiIndex support (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:25735 grouped_window_label).

Findings:
- pandas-observable parity is broken: pandas returns a real MultiIndex(levels=[group, bucket], codes=[..]) which downstream code can index via .loc[("a", "2024-01")] or stack/unstack. A formatted Utf8 label cannot be re-decomposed and breaks group-key extraction in joins/aggregates.
- Tests under r4au were rewritten to assert against the new string format (assert_utf8_index_labels("(a, 2024-01)")), so the fp-frame unit tests are 'green' against the workaround instead of pandas truth.
- New emit-only-value-column path drops the group-key column from the output, so consumers expecting both grouping cols and value cols (the as_index=False contract) now see different shapes depending on as_index gate.

Suggested follow-up:
1. Land genuine row MultiIndex via fp-index::MultiIndex (or document why it's deferred).
2. Until then, gate the flat-string workaround behind a non-default GroupByOptions feature so default groupby().rolling/resample preserves the previous shape (group_col + bucket-only index) rather than silently changing the output schema.
3. Update the r4au tests to mark the flat-string label as a workaround assertion (constant in test scope), not a parity claim.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:03:54.234339370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:19:55.172498265Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:19:55.170529546Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-cj2r","title":"Add Series str title/swapcase/zfill/pad/center/ljust/rjust conformance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:49:24.199348630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:54:13.049744563Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:54:13.047995860Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-cj8ys","title":"fd90.204: Extend prelude compile guard to cover fd90.182-203 additions","description":"fd90.204: Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard to cover all recent prelude additions.

The compile guard test at frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:408 is supposed to ensure every prelude item stays reachable. Its docstring says "If anyone removes one of these from the prelude, this test refuses to compile." But the test was last updated for fd90.155 and doesn't reference items added since then:

- fd90.182: From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> for Scalar (.into() ergonomics)
- fd90.192: DataFrameColumnInput
- fd90.201: MultiIndexOrIndex
- fd90.202: ColumnError, ExprError, FrameError, IndexError, IoError (5 of 7 — TypeError + GroupByError were referenced via crate::, not prelude)
- fd90.203: ValidityMask

Also the test imports `GroupByError` and `TypeError` via `use crate::{GroupByError, TypeError};` (line 409) — that's stale comment behaviour; both are now in the prelude per fd90.202 so the import should be removed.

Scope:
- Remove the stale `use crate::{GroupByError, TypeError};` import
- Add explicit name-checks for: DataFrameColumnInput, MultiIndexOrIndex, ColumnError, ExprError, FrameError, IndexError, IoError, ValidityMask
- Add `.into()` round-trip lines for each Scalar From impl

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs (single test, ~25 lines added).

Removed the stale 'use crate::{GroupByError, TypeError}' import — both were already in the prelude. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-cjx","title":"Add fuzz target for Arrow IPC stream parser invariants","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:09:40.662320937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T06:20:25.464206487Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T06:20:25.462489896Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-cs1r","title":"fd90.44: list_foreign_keys must resolve implicit-PK references via parent's primary_key_columns","description":"fd90.29's list_foreign_keys silently drops FKs that use implicit-PK syntax. SQLite's PRAGMA foreign_key_list returns NULL for the 'to' column when the user declared FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES parent (no column list — implicit reference to parent's PK). My filter 'columns.len() != referenced_columns.len()' then skips the whole FK, leaving callers blind to a real foreign key. SQLAlchemy.Inspector.get_foreign_keys resolves this by looking up the parent's PK columns. Fix: when all to_cols in a group are None, look up the parent table's primary_key_columns via the existing trait method (uses fd90.25 default impl which calls table_schema). Single round-trip per implicit-FK. If the parent has no PK or doesn't exist, fall back to the previous skip behavior with a comment about that being unrecoverable. Add tests: implicit-PK single-col FK resolves to parent.pk_col, implicit-PK composite FK resolves to parent's composite PK in order, explicit-col FK still works (no behavior change), parent without PK still falls through to skip.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:34:52.513568901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:37:40.532491723Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:37:40.529998273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","pandas-parity","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-cs81","title":"fd90.26: SqlConnection::max_identifier_length capability probe","description":"Add SqlConnection::max_identifier_length() -> Option<usize> capability probe for backend identifier-length limits (PostgreSQL 63, MySQL 64, MSSQL 128, SQLite unlimited). Default returns None (no limit / unknown). rusqlite override returns None (SQLite has no documented limit). Useful for to_sql validation when emitting auto-generated index/column names that may exceed backend limits. Free-fn sql_max_identifier_length wrapper. Tests cover SQLite-None default, multi-backend stub overrides for PG/MySQL/MSSQL.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:47:01.541325020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:49:33.662089963Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:49:33.658949048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
